stories.headline: US and Iran 'getting a lot closer' to an agreement to end war, Trump says
stories.summary: The US and Iran are close to signing a Pakistan-drafted framework agreement to end the war, with a decision expected within 48 hours, according to officials who spoke in anonymity. The proposal sets a 30-to-60-day window to negotiate details, though nuclear issues are excluded.

stories.headline: US oil producers increase output to capture price surge from Iran war
stories.summary: Drilling expansion follows 40 per cent jump in cost from global supply crunch that has dented president’s approval ratings

stories.headline: Iran 'getting a lot closer' to agreement with US, Trump says
stories.summary: Tehran also signals progress on talks but says the key issue of nuclear weapons is not part of an initial framework it is working on.

stories.headline: US and Iran move closer to extending ceasefire by 60 days, say mediators
stories.summary: Terms of the deal include the gradual reopening of the Strait of Hormuz

stories.headline: US warns Japan of severe delays in Tomahawk deliveries due to Iran war
stories.summary: Missiles could come two years late as Pentagon works to replenish stocks depleted in Middle East military campaign

stories.headline: Pakistan army chief visits Tehran to push for end to US-Israeli war on Iran
stories.summary: Munir met senior Iranian officials on Saturday as Islamabad and Doha push to finalise a memorandum to end the war.

stories.headline: Israeli forces arrest two children in occupied West Bank village
stories.summary: Israeli soldiers arrest two Palestinian children during a raid on al-Mughayyir village in the occupied West Bank.

stories.headline: Thousands evacuated in California over warning toxic chemical tank could explode or spill
stories.summary: Officials warn the tank in Orange County contains a "highly volatile" substance that could lead to an explosion.

stories.headline: Garden Grove Chemical Tank Could Be Inching Toward Explosion, Authorities Say
stories.summary: Firefighters still have few options to prevent a tank of toxic chemicals in Garden Grove, Calif., from leaking or exploding.

stories.headline: China mine death toll at least 82 after gas blast
stories.summary: Xi Jinping urges authorities to ‘spare no effort’ in rescue operation after explosion in northern Shanxi provinceAt least 82 people have been killed in a gas explosion at a coalmine in northern China’s Shanxi province, in the country’s worst mining disaster in 17 years.The explosion happened on Friday at 7.29pm (12.29 BST) while 247 workers were underground at the Liushenyu coalmine in Qinyuan county, the state media agency Xinhua reported. Conti

stories.headline: Police beat and detain Gaza flotilla activists at airport
stories.summary: Police forcibly detained members of the Global Sumud Flotilla and their supporters at Bilbao Airport.

stories.headline: ‘United States of the Middle East?’: Trump posts US flag covering Iran
stories.summary: Latest Truth Social post comes amid 'delicate diplomacy', expert says, as US and Iran indicate progress in talks.

stories.headline: Xi Jinping urges 'all-out rescue' after coal mine explosion kills at least 82 in China
stories.summary: Six emergency rescue teams consisting of 345 personnel have since been sent to the scene, the Xinhua state news agency reported on Saturday.

stories.headline: Gaza flotilla activists allege abuse by Israeli forces while detained
stories.summary: The detainees say they were slapped, hit, humiliated and even subjected to sexual violence, but Israel's prison service says the allegations are false.

stories.headline: UK officials suggested single market for goods with Europe
stories.summary: Industry figures briefed on the move said the idea has not been taken forward at this point, after EU scepticism.

stories.headline: Bloomberg This Weekend 5/23/2026 | Trump Weighs Iran Options, Tracking The Ebola Outbreak
stories.summary: The news doesn’t stop when markets close. Hosts David Gura, Christina Ruffini and Lisa Mateo bring clarity, context and a bit of humor to the weekend’s biggest headlines, LIVE from New York. Joined by GasBuddy Head of Petroleum Analysis Patrick De Haan, The Points Guy Managing Editor Clint Henderson, Refugees International President Jeremy Konyndyk, Journalist and Author “The Almighty Dollar” Brendan Greeley, Former CIA Director US Army (Ret.) Ge

stories.headline: Zelenskyy rebuffs Merz proposal, says Ukraine deserves full EU membership
stories.summary: Volodymyr Zelenskyy has rejected a proposal by German Chancellor Friedrich Merz to grant Kyiv a non-voting "associate membership". Zelenskyy stressed that Ukraine's EU presence must be complete, with full rights.

stories.headline: ‘Every health facility said they were full’: alarm over rapid spread of Ebola in DRC
stories.summary: New strain of virus, aid cuts, and cultural norms around burials and touch add to difficulties in stemming outbreakThe warnings from aid groups and healthcare workers in the Democratic Republic of the Congo have been stark, their calls for coordinated international action impassioned.As the country reels from the return of the Ebola virus, there is growing concern that its fragile healthcare system will struggle to cope with an outbreak that expe

stories.headline: France bans far-right Israeli minister Ben-Gvir over abuse of activists
stories.summary: French Foreign Minister Jean-Noel Barrot said France would not tolerate its nationals being threatened or intimidated.

stories.headline: French mum and partner held until trial after sons abandoned by road in Portugal
stories.summary: The couple, who allegedly left the woman's sons on a roadside in southern Portugal, will remain in custody.

stories.headline: Senegal's president sacks PM and former ally after months-long feud
stories.summary: Bassirou Diomaye Faye also dissolved the government, creating uncertainty amid a debt crisis in the country.

stories.headline: Hungary’s New Premier Warns Predecessor Left Budget ‘Skeletons’
stories.summary: Hungary’s new Prime Minister Peter Magyar said he was hoping for economic growth of about 2% this year but put voters on notice that the state of public finances left by his predecessor made it impossible to make firm forecasts.

stories.headline: Albanese joins coalition of nations calling for an end to Israeli settlement expansion in the West Bank
stories.summary: Australia joins the UK, Italy, France, Germany, Canada, Norway, the Netherlands and New Zealand in condemning illegal settlementsGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastThe Israeli government is undermining stability in the West Bank as settler violence reaches unprecedented levels, a coalition of western countries says, as its leaders call for an end to construction of Israeli settlements it says breach international law.In a

stories.headline: French Open has ‘positive’ meeting with players over tennis dispute
stories.summary: Roland Garros director discusses greater revenue sharing with player representatives after boycott-impacted media day.

stories.headline: Bond slump stirs vigilante fears
stories.summary: A sense is setting in among investment professionals that we have a serious problem here

stories.headline: SpaceX launches redesigned Starship in successful pre-IPO test
stories.summary: Elon Musk’s company demonstrates the technology at the heart of the biggest initial public offering in history

stories.headline: Uber Proposes Delivery Hero Takeover in €33-Per-Share Offer
stories.summary: Uber Technologies Inc. proposed a takeover offer to buy Delivery Hero SE stock it doesn’t already own for €33 ($38) per share as it seeks to better compete with DoorDash Inc. outside the US.

stories.headline: Trump’s 3,711 Trades Point to Multiple Stock-Market Strategies
stories.summary: President Donald Trump’s latest financial disclosure has drawn scrutiny for its astonishing scale: 3,711 trades, almost entirely in shares of companies across America, including many whose fortunes can turn on federal policy. Bloomberg News US Equities Team Leader Elena Popina joins David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Will the Dollar Remain King in a Cashless Society?
stories.summary: The dollar has been the world's reserve currency for decades, but it's history goes back centuries – to before the United States was  the United States. Brendan Greeley, author of the new book "The Almighty Dollar: 500 Years of the World's Most Powerful Money," joined David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss the currency's history, explain its dominance in global finance, and look ahead to the role it'll play in a wor

stories.headline: Bangladesh Central Bank Launches $5 Billion Fund to Spur Growth
stories.summary: Bangladesh’s central bank unveiled a 600 billion-taka ($5 billion) package to boost economic growth, revive shuttered factories and support small businesses.

stories.headline: Gas Prices Rise as Summer Holiday Travel Begins
stories.summary: Memorial Day weekend is expected to kick off one of the busiest summer travel season in years, with millions of Americans hitting the roads and airports despite rising costs. GasBuddy's Head of Petroleum Analysis Patrick De Hahn and The Points Guy Managing Editor Clint Henderson joined Christina Ruffini and David Gura on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss the climbing gas prices, expensive airfare and what travelers can expect this summer. (Source

stories.headline: Japan's Akazawa has Brief Talk with China's Wang at APEC
stories.summary: Japanese Trade Minister Ryosei Akazawa and Chinese Commerce Minister Wang Wentao spoke briefly Friday at an Asia-Pacific Economic Cooperation dinner in Suzhou, China. The encounter marks the first face-to-face ministerial interaction between the nations since Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi’s comments on Taiwan strained ties. Akazawa confirmed the meeting at a press conference but declined to disclose what was discussed. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: ECB Hike May Be Inevitable to Keep Credibility, Stournaras Says
stories.summary: Preserving the European Central Bank’s credibility is a strong argument in favor of an interest-rate increase next month, according to Governing Council member Yannis Stournaras.

stories.headline: India Raises Diesel, Gasoline Prices for Third Time in Eight Days
stories.summary: India’s state-run refiners raised retail prices again of diesel and gasoline on Saturday to help processors cut losses on discounted sales and to control a spike in demand.

stories.headline: Indonesia Plans to Beat Global Trading Giants at Their Own Game
stories.summary: For years, Indonesia’s raw materials have been ferried from remote mines and plantations to global markets by armies of traders who handle negotiations, loans and even cranes and river barges.

stories.headline: How $6 Gas Prices Are Affecting the Lives of Californians
stories.summary: California is the nation’s most expensive state for gasoline. The latest spike has forced more residents to change their habits.

stories.headline: Jacinta Allan launches state-sponsored apprenticeships at Victoria’s Labor conference ahead of election
stories.summary: The premier is offering 2,000 apprenticeships at the revived State Electricity Commission as part of a pre-election pushGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastJacinta Allan has used Labor’s final state conference before the election to announce a plan for a government-owned electricity body to hire apprentices to address significant workforce shortages and job insecurity.The Victorian premier announced the plan in a speech tha

stories.headline: Anthony Albanese visibly emotional after defending Labor’s capital gains tax and negative gearing changes
stories.summary: Prime minister launches impassioned argument in favour of much-maligned reforms announced in last week’s federal budgetGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastA visibly emotional Anthony Albanese has launched an impassioned defence of Labor’s proposed changes to negative gearing, the capital gains tax discount and family trusts, saying he will “not allow Australia to become a country where aspiration is only for some”.The prime

stories.headline: Tens of thousands rally in Serbia for antigovernment demonstrations
stories.summary: The student-led movement, which began after the Novi Sad disaster in November 2024, is pushing for early elections.

stories.headline: Thousands march in Madrid demanding Spanish PM quit over graft allegations
stories.summary: Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez maintains the cases against his family and entourage are politically motivated.

Pakistan to deploy paramilitary force to guard copper-gold belt
AI looms over Japan's near-record employment rate for new grads
Southeast Asia's turn to crops for fuel leaves less for food and exports

Global infrastructure funding doubles over 5 years, led by Japanese banks
US navy chief says $14bn arms sale to Taiwan paused due to Iran war
stories.summary: Hung Cao told a Senate hearing they were "doing a pause" on the sale to make sure they had munitions for the Iran war.

stories.headline: US arms sales to Taiwan on ‘pause’ due to Iran war, says acting navy chief
stories.summary: Hung Cao’s comments are latest blow to Taipei after Donald Trump recently cast doubt over US’s enduring supportUS arms sales to Taiwan have been “paused” to ensure the US military has enough munitions for its Iran operations, according to Washington’s acting navy secretary, in the latest blow to Taipei after a series of comments by Donald Trump.When asked at a congressional hearing on Thursday about a $14bn (£10.4bn) weapons package awaiting Trum

stories.headline: Suspected Ebola cases triple in a week as WHO warns of rapid spread in DRC
stories.summary: Situation described as ‘deeply worrisome’ by officials as aid cuts and community distrust impede respondersThe Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo poses a “very high” risk to the country, the World Health Organization said on Friday, revising its threat assessment upwards.The outbreak is spreading rapidly, WHO leaders said, with almost 750 suspected cases and 177 suspected deaths, up from 246 cases and 65 deaths when it was fir

stories.headline: PM says Alberta ‘essential’ to Canada as separatists push for independence
stories.summary: Alberta premier calls for referendum on secession after judge ruled initiative to force binding vote invalid The Canadian prime minister, Mark Carney, has said that Alberta is “essential” to the country’s future, hours after the province’s leader moved the oil-rich region closer toward a referendum on independence.Separatists in the western province spent months collecting signatures seeking to trigger a binding October vote on seceding from the

stories.headline: Carney says Alberta is 'essential' to Canada as province plans vote on separation
stories.summary: Alberta's decision to hold a referendum on separation has been widely criticised, including by separatists who say the question is not definitive enough.

stories.headline: Sabalenka cuts media short as French Open players protest
stories.summary: Leading players, including Aryna Sabalenka, Coco Gauff and Jannik Sinner, limit their French Open media duties to 15 minutes in a row over prize money.

stories.headline: AI used to fake evidence that ended Korean actor's career, say police
stories.summary: Police are seeking an arrest warrant for a YouTuber who allegedly fabricated evidence to defame actor Kim Soo-hyun.

stories.headline: Record 274 climbers scale Everest via Nepal in one day
stories.summary: The summit bonanza comes after a late start to this climbing season due to earlier route obstructions.

stories.headline: Rubio doubtful of diplomacy with Cuba as Trump renews threat of military action
stories.summary: US secretary of state says president would like a negotiated agreement with Havana but likelihood ‘is not high’The US president, Donald Trump, and the secretary of state, Marco Rubio, on Thursday again raised the spectre of military intervention in Cuba, a renewed threat that takes on greater weight a day after the administration announced criminal charges against Raúl Castro, the island’s former leader.“Other presidents have looked at this for 5

stories.headline: UN’s climate crisis vote shows political momentum is growing, say experts
stories.summary: Resolution backed by 141 states hailed as ‘new chapter’ that could improve climate diplomacy and litigation effortsWhen the UN general assembly voted overwhelmingly in favour of a landmark climate crisis ruling on Wednesday, the Pacific island of Vanuatu’s prime minister hailed the result as the start of “a new chapter” in climate action.“The task before all of us now is to translate legal clarity into meaningful action, stronger cooperation, and

stories.headline: Weather tracker: Heavy rain triggers flooding and landslides in parts of China
stories.summary: Twelve dead as southern and central areas also suffer travel disruption, electricity outages and evacuationsHeavy, prolonged rainfall in southern and central China has resulted in weather warnings for flash flooding, landslides and waterlogging. The slow-moving band of rainfall spanned 620 miles (1,000km) and travelled eastwards across the regions on Tuesday. It was formed from the convergence of multiple bands of rain originating from the Bay of

stories.headline: UK pitched single market for goods with EU in pursuit of deeper trade ties
stories.summary: Exclusive: Top British official presented idea in Brussels but sources say it was rebuffedThe UK government pitched the creation of a single market for goods with the EU as the cornerstone of an ambitious attempt to reintegrate British trade back into Europe, the Guardian can reveal.During recent visits to Brussels, the Cabinet Office’s top official on EU relations, Michael Ellam, presented the idea to deepen the UK’s economic relationship with t

stories.headline: Rubio tries to reassure Nato allies over US troop deployments
stories.summary: President Donald Trump says he wants to send more troops to Poland, a week after his officials cancelled a similar deployment.

stories.headline: Judge dismisses criminal case against Kilmar Abrego Garcia
stories.summary: A federal judge says the Maryland man who was wrongfully deported last year is being prosecuted for political reasons.

stories.headline: Turkish opposition fights court ousting of leaders in ruling boosting Erdoğan
stories.summary: The appeal court declared Özgür Özel's party leadership victory void, further cementing the president's grip on power.

stories.headline: 'No means no': Greenlanders protest against Trump outside new US consulate
stories.summary: The inauguration of the new consulate comes amid a push by the US president for greater control over the island.

stories.headline: ‘Long live Raúl!’ - Castro’s supporters in Cuba rally in his defence
stories.summary: BBC's Will Grant reports from a pro-government demonstration held after the US indictment of the former president.

stories.headline: Equatorial Leans Against Bidding on Copasa Privatization
stories.summary: Equatorial SA is unlikely to bid on a key stake in Copasa, the Brazilian water utility that’s being privatized by the government of Minas Gerais state, according to people familiar to the matter.

stories.headline: A Ford Truck, Home to Newborn Robins, Is Stuck at a Kansas Car Dealer
stories.summary: Employees of a dealership in Olathe, Kan., found the nest, which is protected under federal law, on top of one of the truck’s tires.

stories.headline: Texas runoff roiled by shadowy spending and a call to imprison ‘American Zionists’
stories.summary: Incendiary comments by a House candidate in Texas prompted condemnation from her fellow Democrats, who accused Republicans of propping her up.

stories.headline: Far-right Israeli minister condemned for taunting handcuffed Gaza flotilla activists
stories.summary: France and Italy are among the countries that have criticised a video showing Itamar Ben-Gvir taunting dozens of activists detained at an Israeli port.

stories.headline: EasyJet boss says summer flights won't be hit by jet fuel shortages
stories.summary: The airline has seen no problems with fuel, but says people are booking later because of uncertainty caused by the Iran war.

stories.headline: From AI to interceptors, Ukraine is trying to drone-proof its skies
stories.summary: After four years of Russia's full-scale invasion, Ukraine has become far more successful at fighting off air raids.

stories.headline: Papua New Guinea warns against fishing in New Ireland after mystery deaths of marine life
stories.summary: Initial testing found evidence of metals in water samples, months after province’s residents began reporting unusual numbers of dead fish washing ashorePapua New Guinea’s government has warned communities not to fish from parts of the New Ireland coastline as preliminary tests show evidence of metals in some water samples, after months of residents reporting dead marine life in the area.On 7 May the fisheries minister, Jelta Wong, said initial te

stories.headline: Jailed Vietnamese tycoon's Birkin bags sell for more than $550K
stories.summary: The disgraced tycoon is serving a life sentence for embezzling from a major Vietnamese bank.

stories.headline: Lebanon says 21 killed, including children, in Israeli air strikes
stories.summary: Twelve people were killed in a single strike on a house, including three children and three women, Lebanon's state-run news agency says.

stories.headline: Rosenberg: Putin enjoys Xi's Chinese welcome but heads home without pipeline deal
stories.summary: Russia and China showed they were shoulder-to-shoulder on the world stage, but it became clear there are limits, says the BBC's Russia Editor.

stories.headline: Are Putin and Xi best friends?
stories.summary: Two BBC correspondents break down the China-Russia relationship.

stories.headline: Norwegian journalist's question to India's Modi sparks online backlash
stories.summary: Journalist Helle Lyng asked the Indian Prime Minister why he does not take questions from the press.

stories.headline: Could a football match soften North Korea-South Korea relations?
stories.summary: A North Korean women's football team played in South Korea marking the first time athletes from the North have crossed the border in nearly 8 years.

stories.headline: Xi basks in spotlight as he hosts Putin days after Trump
stories.summary: Two presidential visits days apart is how Xi Jinping wants the world to see him: talking to everyone, tied to no-one.

stories.headline: The deadly plane attack at the centre of Castro's indictment
stories.summary: Raúl Castro was armed forces minister when Cuban military jets shot down two civilian planes operated by exiles of the communist country.

stories.headline: Anti-government demonstrators and police clash in Bolivia
stories.summary: Protesters have been calling for the president to resign after weeks-long unrest.

stories.headline: Honduran ex-president controversially pardoned by Trump speaks to BBC
stories.summary: Juan Orlando Hernández was serving a 45-year prison sentence for drug trafficking when he was pardoned by Trump.

stories.headline: 'This may be the last time you hear my voice': Political executions surge in Iran since start of war
stories.summary: Since the US and Israel attacked Iran on  28 February, the UN says it’s verified the execution of at least 32 political prisoners.
